name: IDecisionStore
description: Contract for structured decision persistence implementations. Records decisions made during workflow execution with intent, options, rationale, and chosen outcome. Supports querying decision history for audit trails and decision-centric runtime patterns.

methods.name: append

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Unique identifier of the workflow execution

methods.description: Append a decision record to the workflow's decision history. Decisions are append-only — existing records are never modified.

methods.name: query

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Filter by workflow ID. If null, queries across all workflows.

params.name: step_id
params.description: Filter by step ID within the workflow.

params.name: since
params.description: Filter decisions after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

params.name: until
params.description: Filter decisions before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

methods.description: Query decision history with optional filters. All filter parameters are optional — omitting all returns the full history. Results are ordered chronologically (oldest first).

methods.name: delete

params.name: workflow_id
params.description: Unique identifier of the workflow execution to clean up

methods.description: Remove all decision records for a workflow. Called during cleanup. Idempotent — deleting non-existent records is a no-op.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
All methods are async — storage backends may involve I/O operations

Decision is a structured record with: id (str), intent (str), options (list[str]), chosen (str), reasoning (str), outcome (str | None), step_id (str | None), workflow_id (str | None), timestamp (str | None)
append() is append-only — existing decisions must never be modified or overwritten
query() with no filters must return all decisions (full history)
query() results must be ordered chronologically (oldest first)
delete() must be idempotent — deleting non-existent records must not raise
Concurrent appends for the same workflow_id must be safe (ordering preserved)
